mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment rate above 8% signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8%, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate robust supply-side activity that supports corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1) in out-of-sample tests, or returns are negative when both proxies rise.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in residential sales combined with rising housing starts signals overbuilding in the real estate sector, creating headwinds for equity markets that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS declines and HOUST rises, or the coefficient on the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in CPI inflation below 2% signals accommodative monetary policy expectations that boost equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains below 2%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns is less than 0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment signals a tightening labor market that pressures wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no significant predictive power (p > 0.1) or SPX returns are positive when both proxies indicate labor market tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP indicates strong aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The quarterly GDP growth fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of cases, or the coefficient on GDPC1 is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production combined with stable inflation signals strong supply-side dynamics that support equity market gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1) or returns are negative when both proxies rise.
